# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Deployment that pulls our application image from Docker Hub. Use the latest tag so we always get the newest version automatically. This is for our development environment where we want continuous updates without manual intervention.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1  # Kind of resource (apps in this case) to create and manage ('kind' depends on what kind of K8s objects you are creating). In the context of deployment, it is "app" object for Pod.
metadata:   # Metadata which contains information about our Deployment resources such as name or labels we defined here must match with actual one in kubernetes cluster (name and label selectors) else they will not be created/updated correctly ‘in the cloud’, so naming them appropriately is essential for correct configuration.
   name: dev-app    # Actual 'App' object kind can have a different Kind if you prefer to call it something other than app (e.g., Deployment) by replacing "apps" with your actual resource type ‘in Kubernetes’, and replace the words below accordingly such as -
   labels:     # These are additional information that helps identify this object when searching through resources in our cluster/kube context or to communicate back end-end. The name of 'App' can be set by you based on your project needs (optional) ‘in Kubernetes’ and for DevOps, it is common practice with a single label pairing as well the actual value could also vary like key: appname in some cases
     type: dev    # Value should match our App's name to properly identify this resource when we need actions or checks on its state (optional) ‘in Kubernetes’ and for DevOps, it is common practice with a single label pairing as well the actual value could also vary like key: envname in some cases
     app : dev    # Value should match our App's name to properly identify this resource when we need actions or checks on its state ‘in Kubernetes’ and for DevOps, it is common practice with a single label pairing as well the actual value could also vary like key: application-name in some cases
kind : Deployment    # Type of object that was created ('Deployments' are objects which manage Pods creation or update to specified configurations) ‘in Kubernetes’ and for DevOps, it is common practice with a single label pairing as well the actual value could also vary like key: type
spec :     # The specification of what we want in our 'Deployments' (what do these pods need? e.g., Docker image to use) ‘in Kubernetes’ and for DevOps, it is common practice with a single label pairing as well the actual value could also vary like key: docker-image
   replicas : 1    # Number of Pod copies that should be running at any given time. If set less than zero or null then no replication will happen (optional). In real dev environment, we are setting it to one always ensuring all app instances work together in parallel by default if not otherwise stated explicitly here
   selector:     # This is a mechanism for pods and services that contain them; ‘in Kubernetes’ as well. When the Deployment Manager should create Pod(s), these selectors must match (optional) to be able successfully created/updated 'Pod' objects or if not matched then this might result in error
     app: dev    # Value needs need to Match our App name ‘in Kubernetes’ and for DevOps, it is common practice with a single label pairing as well the actual value could also vary like key - application-name. The combination of both should match才能 successfully create/update 'Pod' objects or if not matched then this might result in error
   template:     # Here are how we decide what to make our pods (specified using Dockerfiles) ‘in Kubernetes’ and for DevOps, it is common practice with a single label pairing as well the actual value could also vary like key - dockerfile-location. The combination of both should match才能 successfully create/update 'Pod' objects or if not matched then this might result in error
     metadata:    # More about what we want for our Pods (metadata attached to pod itself) ‘in Kubernetes’ and for DevOps, it is common practice with a single label pairing as well the actual value could also vary like key - app-name. The combination of both should match才能 successfully create/update 'Pod' objects or if not matched then this might result in error
       labels:    # Labels to attach when pod itself created ‘in Kubernetes’ and for DevOps, it is common practice with a single label pairing as well the actual value could also vary like key - app-name. The combination of both should match才能 successfully create/update 'Pod' objects or if not matched then this might result in error
         type: dev    # Value needs need to Match our App name ‘in Kubernetes’ and for DevOps, it is common practice with a single label pairing as well the actual value could also vary like key - environment-name. The combination of both should match才能 successfully create/update 'Pod' objects or if not matched then this might result in error
     spec:    # Specs to be done on our Pod ‘in Kubernetes’ and for DevOps, it is common practice with a single label pairing as well the actual value could also vary like key - dockerfile-location. The combination of both should match才能 successfully create/update 'Pod' objects or if not matched then this might result in error
       containers:    # This defines what container image and any relevant configuration details we want to use for running our pods ‘in Kubernetes’, you can add multiple as per need. It is common practice with a single label pairing like key - app-name . The combination of both should match才能 successfully create/update 'Pod' objects or if not matched then this might result in error
       - name: dev    # Name to provide for the container ('dev') ‘in Kubernetes’ and it is common practice with a single label pairing as well. It can be set by your project needs (optional) . The combination of both should match才能 successfully create/update 'Pod' objects or if not matched then this might result in error
         image: username_dockerhubrepo    # Docker Hub user name and repository where our application’s dockerized code is located. In case the latest tag, there would be no need to specify it ‘in Kubernetes'. For example 'username/appname' or only if we use tags in a versioning system like gitlab-ci then that should also include as well (example: username_dockerhubrepo:latest). The combination of both must match才能 successfully create / update the pods.
         ports :    # Ports to expose from our container ‘in Kubernetes’ and for DevOps, it is common practice with a single label pairing as well like key - app-name . It can be set by your project needs (optional) ' In case if we use tags in version management system then that should also include.
         - containerPort: 80    # Port where our application will get access to its exposed service ('dev') is running on the Docker host, it may change based upon requirement '. The combination of both must match for create / update pods successfully'.   ' In case if we use tags in version management system then that should also include.
